properties.name: Yodel-Ay-Hi-Score! -W/Audio
properties.description:
You are a yodeler. You are also not very good at it. The mountain stares.
Scream into your mic to jump. Sustain a note to float gracefully like the majestic alpine legend you absolutely are not. Go silent and fall like a disappointment. Rocks will fall on you. You will deserve it.
Reach the summit. Achieve glory. Disturb everyone within earshot.

🎤 Microphone HIGLY RECOMMENDED. Headphones and dignity optional.
Made with GDevelop. Powered entirely by your inability to carry a tune.

How to play:

• Scream, yodel, or make any loud noise into your microphone to jump

• Use arrow keys or on-screen controls to move left and right

• Yodel your way to the summit using your microphone or use the spacebar if your neighbors are asleep.

• Time it just right to get an extra BOOST UP!​

• Collect flowers, mushrooms, and stars hidden across each level

• Dodge falling rocks on your way up

• Reach the top!

• Playable by literally anyone who can make noise — toddlers included

**Currently working on mobile controls



🛠 Made with: GDevelop, Web Audio API, pixel art assets, and a lot of screaming.



Best played with headphones off and volume up. Your neighbors will understand.



🤖 A note on AI tools: AI was used in the development of this game, including AI-generated pixel art assets and coding assistance. All game design, level design, creative direction, and final implementation decisions were made by the developer.
properties.author: Syzygy Entertainment

objects.name: CongratulationsText
objects.string: Congratulations, you screamed (cough) I mean yodeled your way to the top and beyond!!!

content.text: Congratulations, you screamed (cough) I mean yodeled your way to the top and beyond!!!

eventsFunctionsExtensions.name: MicrophoneInput
eventsFunctionsExtensions.shortDescription: Read microphone volume amplitude in HTML5 browser exports.
description: Request microphone permission and read normalized microphone volume from 0 to 1.
This extension uses the browser Web Audio API and is intended for HTML5 browser exports only.

inlineCode: throw new Error("getUserMedia is not supported in this browser.");
throw new Error("AudioContext is not supported in this browser.");

eventsFunctions.description: Trigger the browser microphone permission prompt and start reading volume.
eventsFunctions.name: RequestPermission

eventsFunctions.description: Return the current normalized microphone volume from 0 to 1.
eventsFunctions.name: Volume

eventsFunctions.description: Check whether the current normalized microphone volume is above the threshold.
eventsFunctions.name: VolumeIsAbove

eventsFunctionsExtensions.name: PanelSpriteButton
description: A button that can be used for menus and most labelled buttons of a game. The button can be customized with a background for each state and a label. It handles user interactions and a simple condition can be used to check if it is clicked.
There are ready-to-use buttons in the asset-store [menu buttons pack](https://editor.gdevelop.io/?initial-dialog=asset-store&asset-pack=menu-buttons-menu-buttons).

changelog.breaking: - Buttons now use "variants", allowing easy swapping of their visual aspect. You will have to make some adjustments to existing buttons in your project. Follow this [migration documentation](https://wiki.gdevelop.io/gdevelop5/objects/custom-objects-prefab-template/migrate-to-variants/) to do these changes.

events.comment: The "Validated" state only last one frame.
Make sure the cursor position is only checked once per frame.
Touches are always pressed, so ShouldCheckHovering doesn't matter.

eventsFunctionsExtensions.author: @4ian
eventsFunctionsExtensions.name: LinearMovement
eventsFunctionsExtensions.shortDescription: Move objects on a straight line.
description: Move objects on a straight line or according to their angle.

It can be used for simple enemies or bullets. It's usually not adapted for players (other behaviors are a better choice) or bullets fired with the actions provided by the "Fire Bullet" behavior (these bullets are already moved using a force).

eventsBasedBehaviors.description: Move objects on a straight line.
eventsBasedBehaviors.name: LinearMovement

eventsFunctions.name: SpeedY

eventsFunctions.description: the speed of the object.
eventsFunctions.name: Speed
